unit.note: F0 full suite: 4374 passed, 11 skipped, 26 deselected. 2 failures triaged: test_artifact_path_canon[planning-migrated] was REAL (bare 'planning/' in four prose comments) and is fixed; test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact is the known main-root false-fail on ANOTHER iterate's gitignored decision drop (iterate-2026-07-18-fr-existence-gate), not this run's. CI round 1 went red on test_artifact_path_canon for a bare directory-name literal in a guard test: the canon gate scans only GIT-TRACKED files, so a newly split-out module is invisible to it locally until the commit stages it. Fixed by deriving the name from the corpus constant; re-verified with the file tracked. CI round 3 exposed the real lesson: the baseline was carrying THREE platform-specific tokens, not one. The adversarial review caught path separators; the same root cause survived in the type field (pathlib instantiates WindowsPath vs PosixPath) and would have surfaced again in the exception field (reading a directory raises PermissionError on Windows, IsADirectoryError on Linux). Fixed the CLASS: platform tokens are neutralized in the matrix, each platform-dependent fact is pinned in a dedicated test so the normalization launders nothing, and a guard test now forbids any such token from re-entering the baseline.

integration.note: 285 passed, 2 deselected. 49 of them are this iterate's new requirements-corpus suites.

e2e.note: no UI or runtime surface: the diff is test-only and adds no route, component, or executable entry point.

mutation_verification.note: The harness was validated by mutating production and checking it bites. Mutation A (parser accepts lowercase priority) initially SURVIVED, exposing a real corpus gap -- no fixture carried a lowercase priority. Gap closed; A is now caught and transitively catches fr_gates. Mutation B (body column 2 vs 4) caught, naming both affected targets. Production restored clean (git diff --stat shared/ empty).

degraded.reason: Known meta-test false-fail: it resolves gitignored decision-drops from the MAIN root, so it reddens on OTHER in-flight iterates' drops. The named drop (iterate-2026-07-18-fr-existence-gate) belongs to REQ-0, not this run. No action taken by design.

degraded.condition: review_runner's inline walk is not behaviourally frozen
degraded.reason: The walk sits inside run_review, which calls an external LLM (reason_code: requires-external-nondeterministic-service). Compensating control: the golden file records a sha256 of the enclosing function plus its line count, so any edit to it is a baseline diff the S2 reviewer must explain.

surface_verification.justification: Test-only diff. No production file is modified (git diff --name-only shows integration-tests/ and .shipwright/planning/ only), so there is no runtime surface to drive. The harness's own correctness is verified empirically by mutation testing rather than by asserting it passes -- see mutation_verification.

behaviors.behavior: review_runner's inline discovery walk
behaviors.evidence: Walk is inline inside run_review, which calls an external LLM. Registered invoke:source_only; the golden file freezes a sha256 of the enclosing function so an edit is still a baseline diff.
